response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with deep experience in web platforms, Node.js/TypeScript, and systems programming in DLang, but the role demands 8+ years of hands-on modern C++ with STL, multi-threaded networked systems, MQTT/IoT, and IEC 62443 secure development in industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis, and he shows no industrial cybersecurity, message-broker-at-scale in C++, or OT-protocol experience. The systems-level skills are adjacent but not the required stack.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec) with async I/O and caching at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring and workshop delivery
Broad long-term engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including event-driven platform design
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago); primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3) experience
Career trajectory centers on web/product engineering, suggesting misalignment with a C++-centric industrial platform role

key_factors.factor: Lack of substantial modern C++ experience
No industrial cybersecurity or IoT protocol background
Proven high-performance systems and concurrency skills (DLang)
